items.title: Start with conservative complexity
items.avoid: num_leaves=256 or max_depth=12 on a first pass with no regularization.
items.prefer: LightGBM: num_leaves=31, min_data_in_leaf≥20. XGBoost: max_depth=4–6, min_child_weight≥1.
items.why: Shallow boosted trees generalize better; complexity is increased only when validation justifies it.
items.example: Baseline: learning_rate=0.05, early_stopping_rounds=30, then grid-search depth/leaves.

items.title: Always use validation early stopping
items.avoid: Fixing n_estimators=500 without eval_set or callbacks.
items.prefer: Pass eval_set (XGBoost) or valid_sets + early_stopping (LightGBM); deploy best_iteration.
items.why: Late trees fit training residuals that do not generalize.
items.example: model.best_iteration after fit — use predict(..., iteration_range=(0, best_iteration)) in XGBoost 2.x.

items.title: Log metrics every boosting round
items.avoid: Reporting only final test score with no training curve.
items.prefer: Record validation AUC/RMSE per iteration in Tracking Metrics; plot train vs val gap.
items.why: Curves reveal overfitting onset and justify early stopping and hyperparameter choices.
items.example: MLflow log_metric('val_auc', auc, step=round_num) inside evals_result callback.

items.title: Match tree_method to data size
items.avoid: Exact greedy tree_method on wide high-cardinality numeric columns.
items.prefer: tree_method='hist' (XGBoost) on large tabular data; tune max_bin / max_bin_num if needed.
items.why: Histogram splits are why these libraries scale — exact search is a footgun on big data.
items.example: 1M-row click log with float user_id hash feature → hist, not exact.

items.title: Benchmark against Random Forest first
items.avoid: Days of GBDT hyperparameter search before a simple RF baseline.
items.prefer: Train RandomForest with reasonable n_estimators; if GBDT beats RF on CV by a meaningful margin, invest in tuning.
items.why: Establishes whether boosting complexity is worth the ops cost on your dataset.
items.example: RF CV AUC 0.84, tuned LightGBM 0.86 → proceed; RF 0.86, LGB 0.855 → stick with RF or revisit features.
